response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers. However, his systems-level work was in DLang rather than modern C++ or Rust, and his only professional C++ experience was a brief seven-month stint at National Instruments a decade ago. Recent roles are TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, and he has no routing-engine or navigation-guidance experience specifically. He could plausibly transfer DLang systems skills to C++, making him worth a conversation but not a strong fit on paper.
strengths: Deep geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, including open-source map-data platforms
Proven low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching, fibers)
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, Linux and infrastructure experience
concerns: Modern C++/Rust requirement unmet: professional C++ is dated (2014-2015) and brief; primary systems language is DLang
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent years focused on full-stack TypeScript product work rather than systems-level data pipelines

key_factors.factor: Low-latency distributed systems experience in DLang
